name: War History
description: Major wars, battles, and military history through the ages

questions.q: On what date did the D-Day landings at Normandy take place?

source.notes: Operation Overlord. Largest amphibious invasion in history. Eisenhower commanded.

questions.q: The Vietnam War ended for US forces in what year?

source.notes: Fall of Saigon April 30, 1975. US combat troops left in 1973 (Paris Peace Accords).

questions.q: Which 18th-century war was fought on American soil over imperial control between Britain and France?

source.notes: Also known as the North American theater of the Seven Years' War (1754-1763).

questions.q: The American Civil War was fought between which years?

source.notes: April 12, 1861 (Fort Sumter) to May 9, 1865. Deadliest war in US history.

questions.q: In which war was the famous 'Battle of the Bulge' fought?

source.notes: Germany's last major WWII offensive (December 1944-January 1945) in Belgium's Ardennes forest.

questions.q: In what year did World War I begin?

source.notes: Triggered by the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand on June 28, 1914.

questions.q: The assassination of which archduke sparked World War I in 1914?

source.notes: Assassinated in Sarajevo by Gavrilo Princip. Heir to the Austro-Hungarian throne.

questions.q: Which 11th of the 11th at 11 marked what in 1918?

source.notes: 11am, November 11, 1918. Now observed as Veterans/Remembrance Day.

questions.q: The 1815 Battle of Waterloo, which ended Napoleon's rule, took place in modern-day which country?

source.notes: Near Waterloo, Belgium. Wellington's coalition defeated Napoleon.

questions.q: In which war was the Bataan Death March?

source.notes: April 1942. Japanese forced ~76,000 US and Filipino POWs to march ~65 miles in the Philippines.

questions.q: Which war included the Battle of the Alamo in 1836?

source.notes: Texas Revolution. 'Remember the Alamo!' became the rallying cry for Texan independence.

questions.q: The Korean War lasted from 1950 to what year?

source.notes: 1953 armistice paused fighting but no peace treaty signed. Technically still ongoing.

questions.q: What year did Japan surrender, ending WWII?

source.notes: V-J Day announced August 15, 1945. Formal surrender on USS Missouri, September 2, 1945.

questions.q: The Crusades were a series of religious wars primarily between Christians and which other group?

source.notes: Christian armies from Europe fought Muslim armies for control of the Holy Land (1095-1291).

questions.q: The 100 Years' War was fought between which two nations?

source.notes: 1337-1453 (actually 116 years). Featured Joan of Arc and famous longbow victories at Agincourt.

questions.q: The first atomic bomb used in war was dropped on which Japanese city?

source.notes: August 6, 1945. Nagasaki bombed August 9, 1945.

questions.q: Which 1962 Cold War event brought the US and USSR closest to nuclear war?

source.notes: 13-day standoff in October 1962. Soviet missiles in Cuba; US blockade averted direct conflict.

questions.q: Which ancient empire was defeated at the Battle of Marathon in 490 BC?

source.notes: Athenian victory. Messenger Pheidippides's run from battlefield to Athens inspired the marathon race.

questions.q: Which tank was the main US Sherman tank's Soviet counterpart in WWII?

source.notes: Most-produced tank of WWII. Changed the course of battles on the Eastern Front.

questions.q: The Spanish-American War took place in what year?

source.notes: April-August 1898. Ended with US gaining Puerto Rico, Guam, Philippines, and influence over Cuba.
